title: eryngium nudicaule
intro: Eryngium nudicaule, also known as Sea Holly, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carrot family, characterized by its distinctive spiky flowers and foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Eryngium nudicaule has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to support its medicinal properties.
